technique.name: 3-2-1 Shadow Process
technique.description: A structured felt-sense discharge for working with stuck emotional material. You witness, you move through, you arrive.

passages.title: The Weight
passages.text: Something has landed in your body. You don't know what it is yet — only that it's heavy, or sharp, or too warm. It arrived without being invited. The first move is to stop trying to fix it. Just stand in front of it.

choices.text: I can feel it. Something is here.

passages.title: The Witness
passages.text:
What is the shape of this feeling? Where in your body does it live? Is it dense or diffuse? Is it yours alone, or did it come from somewhere else?

You don't need to answer in words. Just let your body describe it.

choices.text: It's in my chest. Tight. Like a stone wrapped in cloth.
It's in my throat. Something I swallowed and never digested.
It's everywhere. I can't find the edges.

passages.title: Three
passages.text:
Let yourself feel it fully for three breaths.

Don't analyze. Don't move toward resolution. Just let it be there — held, witnessed, without demand.

The first breath: you feel the weight.
The second breath: you feel the weight as yours.
The third breath: you feel the weight as information.

passages.title: Two
passages.text:
Now let yourself imagine the weight leaving.

Not gone — you're not pretending it's gone. Just... moving. Making room for what comes next.

The second breath is permission. The third breath is movement.

passages.title: One
passages.text:
And now — what arrives in the space it leaves?

This is not a trick question. There is no right answer. The space after the weight is the territory of this practice. You stay here for one breath.

choices.text: Something quieter. Not happiness. Just... air.
Tiredness. The weight was being held.
I don't know yet. That's okay.

passages.title: After
passages.text:
The weight has moved — or you have moved around it. Either counts.

What stays with you is the note: you can be with difficult material without needing it to resolve before you act. You can carry what needs to be carried, and let the carrying change over time.
